# SL-SCHEMA-010: google-rich-result-unavailable

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

Google rich-result observation is unavailable for a route that requires it.

## Metadata

- Category: Schema.org (schema-org)
- Default severity: info
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: google
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- google

## Required Evidence

- route-contract
- google-observation-state
- observed-at
- fetched-at

## When This Triggers

Compare rich-result policy with latest Google observation availability and quota
state.

## False Positives

False positives are usually caused by stale or sampled external observations.
Check the observation date, freshness, and provider payload before suppressing.

## How To Fix

Check Google quota/freshness and ensure eligible structured data is available.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/schema/google-rich-result-unavailable

Fixed direction:

- Check Google quota/freshness and ensure eligible structured data is available.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Google integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Google evidence.
